texto: It doesn't mean I'm not thinking it. I always feel a bit defeated when I have to follow up with "I love you too". It's like the sequel to a film: I Love You and I Love You Too. You know the second one's always going to be a predictable reworking of the first.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a James Hannah: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “It doesn't mean I'm not thinking it. I always feel a bit defeated when I have to follow up with "…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, love, relationship pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de trabalho e relationship.

frase_original: It doesn't mean I'm not thinking it. I always feel a bit defeated when I have to follow up with "I love you too". It's like the sequel to a film: I Love You and I Love You Too. You know the second one's always going to be a predictable reworking of the first.
